final_text:
Now I have all the information needed. The `to_python` method in `ModelChoiceField` raises a `ValidationError` without passing `params={'value': value}`, so the `%(value)s` placeholder in the error message is never substituted.

{"file":"django/forms/models.py","class":"ModelChoiceField","method":"to_python","parent_class":"ChoiceField","fix_summary":"The raise ValidationError call needs to pass a params dict containing the submitted value (e.g., params={'value': value}) so that the %(value)s placeholder in the invalid_choice error message is properly substituted."}

parsed_answer.fix_summary: The raise ValidationError call needs to pass a params dict containing the submitted value (e.g., params={'value': value}) so that the %(value)s placeholder in the invalid_choice error message is properly substituted.
